message:
fcp-mesh/iblt: reject extreme cell counts without panicking

`IbltCell::pure_key` previously called `self.count.abs() != 1`, which
panics in debug builds when `count == i32::MIN` (negation overflow) and
silently wraps in release. `IbltCell` is `Deserialize`, so a peer can
transmit a cell with `count: i32::MIN` over the gossip path, where
`Iblt::decode` invokes `pure_key` on every cell and crashes the node.

Compare against ±1 directly so that any out-of-range count is treated
as a non-pure cell, preventing decoding from peeling but never panicking.
Add a regression test that decodes a sketch with both `i32::MIN` and
`i32::MAX` cells.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
